root_text.an4.21:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an4.21:2.1: “Ekamidāhaṁ, bhikkhave, samayaṁ uruvelāyaṁ viharāmi najjā nerañjarāya tīre ajapālanigrodhe paṭhamābhisambuddho.
root_text.an4.21:2.2: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.an4.21:2.4: Kiṁ nu kho ahaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyan’ti?
root_text.an4.21:3.2: ‘aparipūrassa kho ahaṁ sīlakkhandhassa pāripūriyā aññaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.21:3.3: Na kho panāhaṁ passāmi sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya aññaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā attanā sīlasampannataraṁ, yamahaṁ sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.21:4.1: Aparipūrassa kho ahaṁ samādhikkhandhassa pāripūriyā aññaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.21:4.2: Na kho panāhaṁ passāmi sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya aññaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā attanā samādhisampannataraṁ, yamahaṁ sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.21:5.1: Aparipūrassa kho ahaṁ paññākkhandhassa pāripūriyā aññaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.21:5.2: Na kho panāhaṁ passāmi sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya aññaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā attanā paññāsampannataraṁ, yamahaṁ sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.21:6.1: Aparipūrassa kho ahaṁ vimuttikkhandhassa pāripūriyā aññaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.21:6.2: Na kho panāhaṁ passāmi sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya aññaṁ samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā attanā vimuttisampannataraṁ, yamahaṁ sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an4.21:7.2: ‘yannūnāhaṁ yvāyaṁ dhammo mayā abhisambuddho tameva dhammaṁ sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an4.21:8.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, brahmā sahampati mama cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evamevaṁ—brahmaloke antarahito mama purato pāturahosi.
root_text.an4.21:8.2: Atha kho, bhikkhave, brahmā sahampati ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā dakkhiṇaṁ jāṇumaṇḍalaṁ pathaviyaṁ nihantvā yenāhaṁ tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.21:8.4: Yepi te, bhante, ahesuṁ atītamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā tepi bhagavanto dhammaṁyeva sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihariṁsu;
root_text.an4.21:8.5: yepi te, bhante, bhavissanti anāgatamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā tepi bhagavanto dhammaṁyeva sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya viharissanti;
root_text.an4.21:8.6: bhagavāpi, bhante, etarahi arahaṁ sammāsambuddho dhammaṁyeva sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya viharatū’ti.
root_text.an4.21:12.2: Idaṁ vatvā maṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyi.
root_text.an4.21:12.3: Atha khvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmuno ca ajjhesanaṁ viditvā attano ca patirūpaṁ yvāyaṁ dhammo mayā abhisambuddho tameva dhammaṁ sakkatvā garuṁ katvā upanissāya vihāsiṁ.
root_text.an4.21:12.4: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, saṅghopi mahattena samannāgato, atha me saṅghepi gāravo”ti.

translation_text.an4.21:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an4.21:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an4.21:2.1: “Mendicants, this one time, when I was first awakened, I was staying in Uruvelā at the goatherd’s banyan tree on the bank of the Nerañjarā River.
translation_text.an4.21:2.2: As I was in private retreat this thought came to mind:
translation_text.an4.21:2.3: ‘One without respect and reverence lives in suffering.
translation_text.an4.21:2.4: What ascetic or brahmin should I honor and respect and rely on?’
translation_text.an4.21:3.2: ‘I would honor and respect and rely on another ascetic or brahmin so as to complete the entire spectrum of ethics, if it were incomplete.
translation_text.an4.21:3.3: But I don’t see any other ascetic or brahmin in this world—with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans—who is more accomplished than myself in ethics, who I should honor and respect and rely on.
translation_text.an4.21:4.1: I would honor and respect and rely on another ascetic or brahmin so as to complete the entire spectrum of immersion, if it were incomplete.
translation_text.an4.21:4.2: But I don’t see any other ascetic or brahmin … who is more accomplished than myself in immersion …
translation_text.an4.21:5.1: I would honor and respect and rely on another ascetic or brahmin so as to complete the entrie spectrum of wisdom, if it were incomplete.
translation_text.an4.21:5.2: But I don’t see any other ascetic or brahmin in this world … who is more accomplished than myself in wisdom …
translation_text.an4.21:6.1: I would honor and respect and rely on another ascetic or brahmin so as to complete the entire spectrum of freedom, if it were incomplete.
translation_text.an4.21:6.2: But I don’t see any other ascetic or brahmin in this world … who is more accomplished than myself in freedom …’
translation_text.an4.21:7.2: ‘Why don’t I honor and respect and rely on the same teaching to which I was awakened?’
translation_text.an4.21:8.1: And then the divinity Sahampati, knowing my train of thought, vanished from the realm of divinity and appeared in front of me, as easily as a strong man would extend or contract his arm.
translation_text.an4.21:8.2: He arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward me, and said:
translation_text.an4.21:8.3: ‘That’s so true, Blessed One! That’s so true, Holy One!
translation_text.an4.21:8.4: All the perfected ones, the fully awakened Buddhas who lived in the past honored and respected and relied on this same teaching.
translation_text.an4.21:8.5: All the perfected ones, the fully awakened Buddhas who will live in the future will honor and respect and rely on this same teaching.
translation_text.an4.21:8.6: May the Blessed One, who is the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha at present, also honor and respect and rely on this same teaching.’
translation_text.an4.21:8.7: That’s what the divinity Sahampati said.
translation_text.an4.21:8.8: Then he went on to say:
translation_text.an4.21:9.4: destroyer of the sorrows of many—
translation_text.an4.21:10.2: they did live, they do live,
translation_text.an4.21:10.4: This is the nature of the Buddhas.
translation_text.an4.21:11.4: remembering the instructions of the Buddhas.’
translation_text.an4.21:12.1: That’s what the divinity Sahampati said.
translation_text.an4.21:12.2: Then he bowed and respectfully circled me, keeping me on his right side, before vanishing right there.
translation_text.an4.21:12.3: Then, knowing the request of the Divinity and what was suitable for myself, I honored and respected and relied on the same teaching to which I was awakened.
translation_text.an4.21:12.4: And since the Saṅgha has also achieved greatness, I also respect the Saṅgha.”

variant_text.an4.21:9.1: ca atītā → ye cabbhatītā (bj, pts1ed, mr)
